Action 1 (Primary): Service restart via SSM Run Command
Action 2 (Fallback): EC2 restart (tag: auto-restart=true) if service restart fails
Action 3 (Final): Escalate to human if EC2 restart fails
Post-Action Verification: HTTP health check on service URL — expect 200 OK within 2 min
Resolution Message: Website was unreachable. Service restarted and verified healthy.
KB Trigger Examples: This Website is Down | Website check failed

Action 1 (Primary): Service restart via SSM Run Command
Action 2 (Fallback): EC2 restart (tag: auto-restart=true) if service restart fails
Action 3 (Final): Escalate to human if EC2 restart fails
Post-Action Verification: HTTP health check on service URL — expect 200 OK within 2 min
Resolution Message: Service health check was failing. Application service restarted and verified healthy.
KB Trigger Examples: CMT Backend API: Backend health check failed | {TRIGGER.NAME}: {STEP.NAME} failed

Action 1 (Primary): Send client communication email with high-memory template
Action 2 (Fallback): Monitor — if memory stays >95% for 30 min: restart EC2 (tag: auto-restart=true)
Action 3 (Final): Escalate to human if memory remains critical after restart
Post-Action Verification: Poll memory utilization via Zabbix API — expect drop below 85% within 5 min
Resolution Message: Memory utilization alert communicated to client. Monitoring for resolution.
KB Trigger Examples: Linux: High memory utilization (>90% for 5m) | Linux: High memory utilization (>98% for 5m)

Action 1 (Primary): Local service restart via SSM: net start AwsReplicationVolumeUpdaterService
Action 2 (Fallback): Full SSM service reset: sc stop → sc start with 60s wait
Action 3 (Final): Escalate to human if service still not running after SSM reset
Post-Action Verification: SSM Run Command: sc query AwsReplicationVolumeUpdaterService — expect RUNNING state
KB Trigger Examples: Windows: "AwsReplicationVolumeUpdaterService" (AwsReplicationVolumeUpdaterService) is not running (startup type automatic)

Action 1 (Primary): Restart Zabbix agent service via SSM (systemctl restart zabbix-agent2)
Action 2 (Fallback): Verify agent connectivity in Zabbix after restart
Action 3 (Final): Escalate to human if still unavailable
Post-Action Verification: Poll Zabbix host availability — expect agent status = available within 3 min
Resolution Message: Zabbix agent service restarted and connectivity restored.
KB Trigger Examples: Linux: Zabbix agent is not available (or nodata for 30m) | Zabbix agent is not available

Action 1 (Primary): Send client email notification with alert-specific template
Action 3 (Final): Monitor — escalate if condition persists >30 min
Post-Action Verification: Monitor trigger status — auto-close ticket if resolved within 30 min
KB Trigger Examples: Windows: High CPU utilization | Linux: High CPU utilization

Alert Pattern (Trigger Match): CPU queue length is too high
Action 1 (Primary): Send client email notification with alert-specific template
Action 3 (Final): Monitor — escalate if condition persists >30 min
Post-Action Verification: Monitor trigger status — auto-close ticket if resolved within 30 min
KB Trigger Examples: Windows: CPU queue length is too high (over 3 for 5m)

Action 1 (Primary): Send client email with disk-space template — include current usage, filesystem, and expansion options
Action 2 (Fallback): Await client approval: client replies specifying X GB increment → human confirms in ticket
Action 3 (Final): After human approval: expand EBS volume by X GB via AWS SDK → extend filesystem → verify new free space
Post-Action Verification: Await human approval response — verify disk utilization drops after volume expansion
Resolution Message: Client approved storage expansion. Volume expanded and filesystem resized successfully.
KB Trigger Examples: Linux: disk space is critically low | Windows: disk space is low

Action 1 (Primary): Identify instance type: check if EC2 or ECS task/container instance
Action 2 (Fallback): If EC2 in ASG: ignore (ASG replaces). If EC2 not in ASG: escalate to human. If ECS task: check if service auto-recovery is enabled — if yes ignore, if no escalate
Action 3 (Final): Create high-priority ManageEngine ticket + urgent SES email for non-ASG/non-auto-recovery terminations
Post-Action Verification: Verify ASG replacement launched (if ASG) or confirm human acknowledged ticket
KB Trigger Examples: AWS EC2: {HOST.NAME} is terminated | AWS ECS: {HOST.NAME} is terminated

Action 3 (Final): Monitor — escalate if condition persists >30 min
Post-Action Verification: Monitor trigger status — auto-close ticket if resolved within 30 min
